meaning: adj.<美俚>非常出色的人(物)的,非常显眼的
examples: Man, that English test was a doozy. I think I failed.
She concocted a doozy to get her husband to see the doctor.
This bubble was a doozy and its pop was felt around the world.
Phinney was competing in Italy's Tirreno-Adriatico stage race, and this penultimate stage was a doozy.
If so, then we have the makings for a doozy of a bubble.
